channel.title: Space Quotes — Tidbits
channel.description: Sourced tidbits from real FCC, ITU and FAA space filings, each built around a quotable line from the filing.

item.title: AST SpaceMobile tells the FCC how it will steer its giant satellites back down
item.description: Before a satellite dies, someone has to plan how it falls. In a February 20, 2026 supplement to the FCC, AST & Science (AST SpaceMobile) replaced a passive p…

item.title: Planet Labs backs faster satellite licensing, with a warning about what would still slow it down
item.description: Planet Labs wants the FCC to license satellites faster, and it has a warning about why faster licensing alone will not be enough. In reply comments filed Feb…

item.title: Starcloud asks the FCC to license a data center in orbit for training AI
item.description: Starcloud, Inc. is asking the FCC for permission to put a data center in orbit, and it is not thinking small. The company's February 2, 2026 license applicat…

item.title: Amazon says the FCC is sharing satellite spectrum by a yardstick from when Ka-band was 'in its infancy'
item.description: Amazon thinks the FCC is refereeing a modern spectrum fight with an outdated rulebook. In a January 29, 2026 letter, its Kuiper Systems unit asked the Commis…

item.title: Rural carriers tell the FCC: don't let satellites beam over our spectrum without permission
item.description: The Competitive Carriers Association represents wireless providers that range, in its words, from "small, rural carriers serving fewer than 5,000 customers" …

item.title: SpaceX wants EchoStar's appeal of its direct-to-cell waiver thrown out
item.description: EchoStar asked the FCC to reverse a waiver the agency's bureaus had granted for SpaceX's satellite-to-phone service. SpaceX notes that EchoStar was the only …

item.title: Astroscale asks the FCC to license a spacecraft that refuels other satellites in orbit
item.description: Astroscale wants to refuel satellites in orbit, and its FCC license application spells out the hardware that would do it. The spacecraft, called APS-R (Astro…

item.title: Amazon urges the FCC to open the 17 GHz band for satellite broadband
item.description: Amazon wants more room for satellite broadband, and it told the FCC the homework is done. In an ex parte letter filed September 24, 2024, its Kuiper Systems …

item.title: Iridium joins SpaceX and Kuiper in asking the FCC for a hard limit on satellites exploding
item.description: Iridium wants the FCC to put a hard number on how often satellites are allowed to blow up. In a June 24, 2024 letter to the orbital-debris docket, it said it…

item.title: Aethero wants to launch a satellite that does its computing in orbit, not on the ground
item.description: Most satellites collect data and beam it down to be processed on the ground. Aethero wants to do the work in orbit. Its March 11, 2024 FCC license applicatio…
